message: bd-321a.1: spec extension session API contract

files.patch:
@@ -145,7 +145,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-1uy.1.1","title":"Hostcall ABI: Implement Shared Dispatcher (HostCallPayload -> HostResultPayload)","description":"# Goal
Make `dispatch_host_call_shared()` (in `src/extensions.rs`) the **single source of truth** for hostcall execution, and harden it so it is:
- **Spec compliant** (schema-valid `host_result`)
- **Strict taxonomy** only (`timeout|denied|io|invalid_request|internal`)
- **Deterministic** (params hashing + logs)
- **Safe** (capability derivation is core-defined; mismatch => `invalid_request`)

This bead is the foundation that unblocks wiring the shared dispatcher into:
- JS runtime (`bd-1uy.1.3`)
- WASM runtime (`bd-nom.1`)
- Protocol adapter (`bd-1uy.1.2`)

# Current State (Observed)
- `dispatch_host_call_shared()` exists and already does:
  - `validate_host_call()`
  - `required_capability_for_host_call()` derivation
  - policy evaluation + prompt-cache integration
  - timeout wrapping via `asupersync::time::timeout`
  - structured logs: `host_call.start`, `policy.decision`, `host_call.end`
- BUT there are correctness/spec gaps that must be fixed *before* it can be the canonical ABI:

## Gap A: `host_result.output` must be an object
- `validate_host_result()` requires `output` is an object.
- The shared helpers currently return `output: Value::Null` on errors.
- Spec (`EXTENSIONS.md` §3.2) explicitly says output is an object and may be empty on error.

### Fix
- Change shared error constructors to always use `json!({})` as output.
- Add regression tests ensuring shared dispatcher results always pass `validate_host_result()`.

## Gap B: Non-taxonomy internal codes must map deterministically
- Internal dispatchers can currently emit non-taxonomy `HostcallOutcome::Error.code` values like:
  - `tool_error`
  - `SHUTDOWN`
  - `CANCELLED`
- Shared dispatcher must map these to taxonomy in a principled way.

### Proposed mapping (v1)
- `tool_error` -> `io` (default), unless there is a clear `invalid_request` signal
- `SHUTDOWN`/`shutdown` -> `internal` (host lifecycle), not `denied`
- `CANCELLED` -> `timeout` (closest user-facing semantics) OR `internal` (if we want to reserve `timeout` strictly for wall deadlines). Choose once, document, test.

## Gap C: Canonical `params` shape for session/ui/events
- Cross-runtime parity requires that the canonical `{method, params}` is identical between JS and WASM for the same logical call.
- WASM hostcalls treat params as a **flattened** object: `{ op: "set_model", provider: "...", modelId: "..." }`.
- JS hostcalls today are split:
  - dispatch uses `payload` (args only)
  - hashing uses `{ op, args }`

### Fix
- Define and enforce the canonical params shape in the shared dispatcher contract:
  - `tool`: `{ name, input }`
  - `exec`: `{ cmd, args?, options? }`
  - `http`: `{ url, method, headers?, body?, body_bytes?, timeout_ms? }`
  - `session/ui/events`: `{ op, ...args_fields }` (flattened)
- The wiring beads (`bd-1uy.1.3`, `bd-nom.1`) must convert their runtime-native requests into this canonical shape before calling `dispatch_host_call_shared()`.

# Tests (Must-Have)
## Unit tests (table-driven)
- Validation:
  - empty call_id -> `invalid_request`
  - unknown method -> `invalid_request`
  - capability mismatch -> `invalid_request`
- Policy:
  - strict deny -> `denied`
  - permissive allow -> success
- Timeouts:
  - timeout_ms triggers `timeout` and sets `error.retryable=true`
- Method coverage (happy-path + one failure each):
  - tool
  - exec
  - http
  - session
  - ui
  - events
  - env
  - log

## Logging assertions
- Emits `host_call.start` and `host_call.end`
- Includes `runtime`, `call_id`, `extension_id`, `capability`, `method`, `params_hash`, and `duration_ms`
- Never logs raw params (hash only)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All `dispatch_host_call_shared()` outputs pass `validate_host_result()`.
- [ ] All error codes are mapped into the fixed taxonomy.
- [ ] Canonical params shape is documented (in-bead) and enforced by tests.
- [ ] Shared dispatcher is ready to be called by JS/WASM/protocol wiring beads without bespoke policy/timeout/logging.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T06:55:42.245710396Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T08:11:23.692829169Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T08:11:23.692684239Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["extensions","hostcall","protocol"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1uy.1.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1uy.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T06:55:42.245710396Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":525,"issue_id":"bd-1uy.1.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Implemented shared hostcall dispatcher (dispatch_host_call_shared) as the single source of truth for all runtimes. Includes HostCallContext struct, 9 method dispatchers (tool/exec/http/fs/env/session/ui/events/log), policy resolution with prompt cache, timeout via asupersync wall-clock, structured logging with params_hash, and strict error taxonomy. 23 unit tests covering validation, policy denial, capability mismatch, timeout, env/fs/log/session methods.","created_at":"2026-02-06T08:04:44Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1uy.1.1.1","title":"Hostcall ABI dispatcher: Spec-valid host_result payloads (output={}, error iff is_error)","description":"# Goal
Fix shared dispatcher result construction so every `HostResultPayload` it emits is **schema-valid** and passes `validate_host_result()`.

# Current Bug / Risk
- `validate_host_result()` requires `output` is an object.
- Shared dispatcher helpers currently use `output: null` on errors (`shared_result_err*`).
- This becomes a latent failure as soon as we wire protocol adapter (`ExtensionMessage host_call -> host_result`) or add parity tests that validate payloads.

# Scope
- Update shared constructors used by `dispatch_host_call_shared()`:
  - `shared_result_err()`
  - `shared_result_err_with_details()`
- Required invariants:
  - `output` is always `json!({})` on error
  - `error` is present iff `is_error=true`
  - `chunk` is None unless explicitly streaming

# Tests
- Unit tests that:
  - call `dispatch_host_call_shared()` on a denied call and assert `validate_host_result()` succeeds.
  - call `dispatch_host_call_shared()` on an invalid_request and assert schema-valid payload.
  - (optional) compile and validate against `docs/schema/extension_protocol.json` for a `host_result` message.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] No shared dispatcher error path returns `output: null`.
- [ ] `validate_host_result()` passes for both success and error results.","status":"in_progress","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:41:00.502778382Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T08:11:50.253417415Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1uy.1.1.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1uy.1.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:41:00.502778382Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1uy.1.1.2","title":"Hostcall ABI dispatcher: Canonical params shape + params_hash parity contract","description":"# Goal
Define and enforce the **canonical hostcall params shape** used for:
- capability derivation (`required_capability_for_host_call`)
- `params_hash` computation
- cross-runtime parity tests (JS/WASM/protocol)

This eliminates a class of “same logical call, different hash/logs” drift.

# Current State / Drift
- WASM hostcalls already treat params for `session/ui/events` as *flattened*:
  - `{ op: "set_model", provider: "...", modelId: "..." }`
- JS hostcalls currently have split representations:
  - dispatch uses args-only payload
  - hashing uses `{ op, args }`

Shared dispatcher expects `HostCallPayload.params` to be the canonical representation.

# Canonical Params Shapes (v1)
- `tool`: `{ name: <tool_name>, input: <tool_input_object> }`
- `exec`: `{ cmd: <string>, args?: <array>, options?: <object>, ... }`
  - accept legacy `command` alias for cmd (normalize)
- `http`: `{ url, method?, headers?, body?, body_bytes?, timeout_ms? }`
- `fs`: `{ op: <read|write|list|stat|mkdir|delete>, path: <string>, ... }`
- `env`: `{ name?: <string>, names?: <string[]> }`
- `session/ui/events`: `{ op: <string>, ...args_fields }` (flattened)
- `log`: `{ level?: <string>, message?: <string>, data?: <any> }`

# Implementation Scope
- Add a helper that can build canonical params for session/ui/events from `(op, args)`:
  - flatten object args into top-level keys
  - preserve non-object args by storing under a reserved key (e.g. `payload`) if needed
- Update/ensure `shared_params_hash()` hashes `{method, params}` using canonicalized JSON (sorted object keys, stable arrays).
- Document the contract in-code near the dispatcher and in-bead so future changes are deliberate.

# Tests
- Table-driven tests verifying that:
  - canonicalization produces stable JSON for equivalent inputs
  - params_hash matches between JS and WASM for the same logical call
  - validate_host_call still succeeds for canonical payloads

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Canonical params shapes are implemented and documented.
- [ ] JS/WASM parity tests can rely on identical params_hash for canonical calls.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:41:27.920222829Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T07:41:27.920222829Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1uy.1.1.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1uy.1.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:41:27.920222829Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"bd-1uy.1.1.3","title":"Hostcall ABI dispatcher: Eliminate non-taxonomy error codes (tool_error/SHUTDOWN/CANCELLED)","description":"# Goal
Ensure the shared hostcall dispatcher is the **single enforcement point** for the fixed v1 error taxonomy:
- `timeout`
- `denied`
- `io`
- `invalid_request`
- `internal`

No other error codes may escape at the ABI boundary (JS, WASM, protocol).

# Current State
- Lower-level JS/WASM dispatch helpers can emit non-taxonomy strings in `HostcallOutcome::Error.code`:
  - `tool_error`
  - `SHUTDOWN`
  - `CANCELLED`
- `outcome_to_host_result()` currently maps unknown codes to `internal`, and maps `shutdown` to `denied`.

# Scope
1. Define the canonical mapping for legacy/internal codes:
   - `tool_error` -> `io` (default)
   - `shutdown`/`SHUTDOWN` -> `internal` (host lifecycle)
   - `cancelled`/`CANCELLED` -> `timeout` or `internal` (choose once; document)
2. Update `outcome_to_host_result()` accordingly.
3. Where practical, remove the sources of these codes in the underlying dispatchers:
   - tool execution errors should directly choose taxonomy codes
   - manager shutdown should return a deterministic taxonomy code

# Tests
- Unit tests:
  - force each legacy code path and assert mapped `HostCallErrorCode` is as expected.
  - assert no code outside taxonomy appears in emitted `HostResultPayload.error.code`.
- Parity tests (`bd-1uy.1.4`) must fail if any runtime emits a non-taxonomy code.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Shared dispatcher maps all known legacy codes into taxonomy deterministically.
- [ ] Unknown codes are treated as `internal` and logged (optional warning) so drift is visible.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:41:44.879713184Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T07:41:44.879713184Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1uy.1.1.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1uy.1.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:41:44.879713184Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
+{"id":"bd-1uy.1.1.3","title":"Hostcall ABI dispatcher: Eliminate non-taxonomy error codes (tool_error/SHUTDOWN/CANCELLED)","description":"# Goal
Ensure the shared hostcall dispatcher is the **single enforcement point** for the fixed v1 error taxonomy:
- `timeout`
- `denied`
- `io`
- `invalid_request`
- `internal`

No other error codes may escape at the ABI boundary (JS, WASM, protocol).

# Current State
- Lower-level JS/WASM dispatch helpers can emit non-taxonomy strings in `HostcallOutcome::Error.code`:
  - `tool_error`
  - `SHUTDOWN`
  - `CANCELLED`
- `outcome_to_host_result()` currently maps unknown codes to `internal`, and maps `shutdown` to `denied`.

# Scope
1. Define the canonical mapping for legacy/internal codes:
   - `tool_error` -> `io` (default)
   - `shutdown`/`SHUTDOWN` -> `internal` (host lifecycle)
   - `cancelled`/`CANCELLED` -> `timeout` or `internal` (choose once; document)
2. Update `outcome_to_host_result()` accordingly.
3. Where practical, remove the sources of these codes in the underlying dispatchers:
   - tool execution errors should directly choose taxonomy codes
   - manager shutdown should return a deterministic taxonomy code

# Tests
- Unit tests:
  - force each legacy code path and assert mapped `HostCallErrorCode` is as expected.
  - assert no code outside taxonomy appears in emitted `HostResultPayload.error.code`.
- Parity tests (`bd-1uy.1.4`) must fail if any runtime emits a non-taxonomy code.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Shared dispatcher maps all known legacy codes into taxonomy deterministically.
- [ ] Unknown codes are treated as `internal` and logged (optional warning) so drift is visible.","status":"in_progress","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:41:44.879713184Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T08:54:15.700460008Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1uy.1.1.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1uy.1.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:41:44.879713184Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1uy.1.1.4","title":"Hostcall ABI dispatcher: Comprehensive unit tests + log ledger assertions","description":"# Goal
Add an exhaustive, table-driven unit test suite for `dispatch_host_call_shared()` that:
- exercises all methods and major error branches
- asserts strict taxonomy
- asserts deterministic structured logs (evidence ledger)

# Why
We are about to make `dispatch_host_call_shared()` the single enforcement point for JS+WASM+protocol. The tests must prevent regressions and encode the ABI contract.

# Scope
- Add table-driven tests covering:
  - `tool` success + unknown tool + tool error
  - `exec` success + invalid_request (args type) + timeout (short timeout)
  - `http` invalid scheme + denied host + success via mock server
  - `session` invalid_request + success via session stub
  - `ui` denied (no manager) + timeout via UI stub
  - `events` representative op(s)
  - `env` invalid_request + denied + success
  - `log` success and emits `extension.log`
- For each case, assert:
  - `validate_host_result()` passes
  - `error.code` is one of the taxonomy
  - success output shape is stable

# Logging Assertions
- Capture `tracing` events in-memory for the test and assert:
  - `host_call.start` logged once
  - `policy.decision` logged once
  - `host_call.end` logged once
  - fields include `runtime`, `call_id`, `capability`, `method`, `params_hash`, `duration_ms`
  - no raw params appear in logged fields

# Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Tests are deterministic and do not require real network.
- [ ] Any drift in taxonomy/log shapes fails tests loudly.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:42:04.908588500Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T07:42:04.908588500Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1uy.1.1.4","depends_on_id":"bd-1uy.1.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:42:04.908588500Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1uy.1.2","title":"Hostcall ABI: Wire ExtensionMessage host_call -> host_result (Protocol Adapter)","description":"# Goal
Make the protocol types in `src/extensions.rs` actionable by implementing a runtime/library **protocol adapter** that:

1. Accepts `ExtensionMessage(type=host_call)`
2. Dispatches via `dispatch_host_call_shared()`
3. Emits `ExtensionMessage(type=host_result)`

This turns `host_call`/`host_result` into a real ABI surface that can be used for:
- harness testing (treat hostcall ABI as an artifact)
- future Tier-C process IPC runtimes
- debugging/replay tooling (log-driven reproduction)

# Current State
- `ExtensionMessage::parse_and_validate()` exists.
- Hostcall validation exists (`validate_host_call`, `validate_host_result`).
- Shared dispatcher exists (`dispatch_host_call_shared`) but is not exposed through protocol message routing.

# Scope
- Provide a small, decoupled API surface (library function), not necessarily a user-facing CLI:
  - `async fn handle_extension_message(ctx: HostCallContext<'_>, msg: ExtensionMessage) -> Vec<ExtensionMessage>`
- v1 supported message types:
  - `host_call` -> `host_result`
- For other message types:
  - either return a single `error` message, OR
  - return a `host_result` with `invalid_request` (pick one and document)

# Required Semantics
- Must preserve `call_id` exactly.
- Must emit schema-valid `HostResultPayload`:
  - `output` is always an object (`{}` on error)
  - `error` present iff `is_error=true`
- Error codes must be taxonomy-only.
- Message `id` rules:
  - Do not reuse incoming message id for the response.
  - Generate a deterministic response id format (e.g. `"host_result:<call_id>"` or `"msg-<uuid>"` depending on determinism needs).
  - Include `context`/correlation only in logs, not in protocol payload unless spec requires.

# Streaming-Ready Design (Future-Proof)
- Return `Vec<ExtensionMessage>` so we can later emit multiple `host_result` chunks (`chunk.index`, `chunk.is_last`).
- Initial implementation emits exactly 1 message with `chunk=None`.

# Tests
- Unit tests:
  - parse+validate `host_call` JSON -> adapter -> `host_result` validates (including `validate_host_result`).
  - capability mismatch -> `invalid_request`.
  - denied-by-policy -> `denied`.
  - timeout -> `timeout` with retryable flag.
- Harness test:
  - use protocol adapter to simulate an external runtime calling hostcalls and compare logs/outputs to JS/WASM parity test suite (`bd-1uy.1.4`).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `ExtensionMessage(type=host_call)` round-trips through adapter to a valid `host_result`.
- [ ] No bespoke policy/timeout/logging exists in the adapter; it is a thin wrapper around the shared dispatcher.
- [ ] Adapter can be used by future process-based extension runners without rewriting hostcall semantics.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T06:56:09.534702397Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T07:36:01.750493646Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["extensions","hostcall","protocol"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1uy.1.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1uy.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T06:56:09.534702397Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1uy.1.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1uy.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T06:56:09.534702397Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1uy.1.3","title":"Hostcall ABI: JS Runtime Uses Shared Dispatcher + Taxonomy","description":"# Goal
Refactor PiJS hostcalls so **all JS-origin hostcalls** execute through the shared hostcall dispatcher:

`HostcallRequest (PiJS)` -> canonical `HostCallPayload` -> `dispatch_host_call_shared()` -> `HostResultPayload` -> Promise completion

This removes JS/WASM drift and enforces the fixed taxonomy.

# Current State (Observed)
- PiJS enqueues `HostcallRequest` from `src/extensions_js.rs`.
- The runtime pump (`pump_js_runtime_once` in `src/extensions.rs`) drains the queue and currently dispatches via `dispatch_hostcall()` / `dispatch_hostcall_allowed()` which:
  - returns `HostcallOutcome`
  - may emit non-taxonomy error codes (`tool_error`, `SHUTDOWN`, `CANCELLED`)
  - logs separately from the shared dispatcher
- Shared dispatcher exists: `dispatch_host_call_shared()`.

# Scope
1. **Canonical payload conversion** (JS request -> HostCallPayload)
   - Set `call_id` from request.
   - Set `method` from request kind:
     - Tool -> `"tool"`
     - Exec -> `"exec"`
     - Http -> `"http"`
     - Session -> `"session"`
     - Ui -> `"ui"`
     - Events -> `"events"`
   - Set `capability` to the derived capability (core-defined):
     - tool name mapping per `required_capability_for_host_call()`
     - other methods use method name (`exec/http/session/ui/events/...`)
   - Set `timeout_ms` from existing JS hostcall timeout extraction (`js_hostcall_timeout_ms`).

2. **Canonical `params` shape alignment** (critical for parity)
   - `tool`: `{ "name": <tool>, "input": <payload> }`
   - `exec`: `{ "cmd": <cmd>, ...payload_fields }` (ensure `args/options` propagate)
   - `http`: params is the request object (existing)
   - `session/ui/events`: flatten op + args into a single object:
     - `{ "op": <op>, ...args_fields }`
     - This matches the WASM hostcall params convention used by `HostState::call()` today.

3. **Dispatch + completion**
   - Build `HostCallContext` with:
     - `runtime = "js"`
     - `extension_id = request.extension_id`
     - `policy`, `tools`, `http`, `manager` (for prompt cache + session/ui/events)
     - `fs/env_allowlist` (only if JS runtime should expose them; otherwise None)
   - Call `dispatch_host_call_shared()`.
   - Convert `HostResultPayload` -> `HostcallOutcome` for Promise completion:
     - success: `HostcallOutcome::Success(result.output)`
     - error: `HostcallOutcome::Error { code: taxonomy_code, message }`

4. **Eliminate old paths**
   - Remove or quarantine the older `dispatch_hostcall*` codepaths that bypass shared dispatcher.
   - Keep test interceptors (if needed) as a deliberate hook *around* the shared dispatcher rather than a parallel implementation.

# Required Semantics
- Preserve deterministic scheduler invariants (no synchronous reentrancy): completions must still be delivered as macrotasks.
- JS-visible error codes must be taxonomy-only.
- Logs must remain deterministic and must not include raw params (hash-only).

# Tests
- Unit tests:
  - tool failure maps to taxonomy (no `tool_error`)
  - manager shutdown path maps to taxonomy (no `SHUTDOWN`)
  - unknown tool name -> `invalid_request`
  - denied-by-policy -> `denied`
  - session/setModel input validation -> `invalid_request`
- Determinism tests:
  - confirm that params_hash matches WASM for identical logical calls (requires canonical params shape)
- Conformance harness smoke:
  - run a JS extension that exercises session/ui/events and assert logs + outcomes match fixtures.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] PiJS hostcalls do not bypass `dispatch_host_call_shared()`.
- [ ] JS Promise rejection codes are taxonomy-only.
- [ ] `params_hash` parity holds for canonical calls vs WASM/protocol.
- [ ] Existing PiJS scheduler/event-loop conformance tests remain green.","status":"in_progress","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T06:56:34.065311493Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T08:06:05.907283707Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["extensions","hostcall","js"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1uy.1.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1uy.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T06:56:34.065311493Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1uy.1.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1uy.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T06:56:34.065311493Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-31j","title":"Generate per-extension conformance report (machine + human)","description":"Background:
- We need a consumable summary of results for stakeholders and future self.

Steps:
- Produce a report that lists each extension, version, runtime tier, and pass/fail.
- Include failure reasons and links to fixtures/logs.
- Ensure report can be regenerated automatically.

Acceptance:
- Report is updated by running a single documented command.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss
- [ ] Unit tests cover core success/failure + edge cases for this bead
- [ ] Integration/E2E script exercises the end-to-end path (or explicitly marks N/A with rationale) and emits detailed JSONL logs + artifacts per bd-4u9
- [ ] Logs include inputs, outputs, timing, and redaction summary; artifacts list is deterministic
- [ ] Quality gates pass: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test
- [ ] Docs/fixtures updated if behavior or UX changes

[ ] Logs include inputs, outputs, timing, and redaction summary; artifacts list is deterministic
[ ] Quality gates pass: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test
[ ] Docs/fixtures updated if behavior or UX changes","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T02:25:23.180071849Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T00:40:22.401800169Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T00:40:22.401662342Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-31j","depends_on_id":"bd-16n","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:00Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-31j","depends_on_id":"bd-1nq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:00Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-320","title":"Implement JS compatibility pipeline (bundle -> runtime)","description":"Background:
- Many extensions are JS/TS; we need a compatibility tier to run them as-is.
- Assume **QuickJS has no WebAssembly**: JS compatibility must be hybrid (QuickJS for JS + wasmtime-backed PiWasm for wasm).

Steps:
- Lock the JS execution strategy for the pinned sample:
  - QuickJS bytecode for JS execution.
  - PiWasm bridge (bd-1ry) for `globalThis.WebAssembly` expectations.
  - JS→WASM is optional/future; do not block sample success on it.
- Implement build pipeline integration + caching by hash (extc/SWC) and artifact metadata.
- Provide/ensure shims for legacy APIs (Node core + globals) map into connector hostcalls.
- Integrate with the QuickJS runtime and connector dispatcher.
- Ensure module loading contract supports shim modules and deterministic resolution.

Acceptance (hard):
- **All 16 extensions in `docs/extension-sample.json` run unmodified** (no manual source edits), end-to-end under the conformance/E2E harness.

Notes:
- Node core shims: bd-3d0 + bd-2sr.
- WebAssembly bridge: bd-1ry.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss
- [ ] Unit tests cover core success/failure + edge cases for this bead
- [ ] Integration/E2E script exercises the end-to-end path (or explicitly marks N/A with rationale) and emits detailed JSONL logs + artifacts per bd-4u9
- [ ] Logs include inputs, outputs, timing, and redaction summary; artifacts list is deterministic
- [ ] Quality gates pass: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test
- [ ] Docs/fixtures updated if behavior or UX changes

 {"id":"bd-321a","title":"Extension session connector integration (interactive + RPC)","description":"# Background
Extensions rely on session operations to:
- read the current session state (model/thinking/streaming flags, session id/name/file)
- enumerate messages/entries/branch
- mutate session metadata and content (set_name, append_message, append_entry, set_model, set_thinking_level, set_label)

In the Hostcall ABI these should be routed as `host_call` with `method = session` and a connector-specific `params` shape.

`src/extensions.rs` defines the `ExtensionSession` trait and `dispatch_hostcall_session()` already supports a rich set of ops + aliases.

# Gap (Concrete, Observed)
We currently have **two** `ExtensionSession` implementations with **different semantics**:
- Interactive: `InteractiveExtensionSession` (`src/interactive.rs`)
- Non-interactive / agent session handle: `SessionHandle` (`src/session.rs`)

Mismatch examples (these are the real parity risks):
- `get_state`:
  - interactive returns a rich object (e.g. `model`, `thinkingLevel`, `isStreaming`, `isCompacting`, `sessionId`, `sessionFile`, `sessionName`, `autoCompactionEnabled`, `messageCount`, ...)
  - `SessionHandle` returns only `{sessionFile, sessionName}`
- `get_messages`:
  - interactive filters to message entries on the current path + specific message types
  - `SessionHandle` returns all `SessionEntry::Message` across the entire session, not branch-scoped
- `get_entries` / `get_branch`:
  - interactive returns entries for the current path; branch is treated as current path
  - `SessionHandle` returns all entries for `get_entries`, but branch is only current path
- Persistence:
  - interactive saves immediately when `save_enabled`
  - `SessionHandle` mutates in-memory but does not save

Net: extensions can observe different state shapes + ordering depending on mode, which breaks compatibility and makes conformance/E2E flaky.

# Goal
Implement the session connector surface used by extensions and wire it end-to-end with **identical semantics** in:
- interactive TUI mode
- RPC mode (`pi --mode rpc`)

# Deliverables
1) **Contract (spec-first)**
- Define exact op names + params + return shapes (including allowed aliases).
- Pin field names + defaults to legacy expectations.

2) **Shared semantics**
- Unify the "extension-visible session state" builder so interactive + RPC produce the same shape.
- Enforce deterministic ordering for enumeration APIs.
- Define persistence rules (when session mutations must be saved) so extensions do not observe "worked but not persisted" behavior.

3) **Hostcall ABI wiring**
- Route `session.*` through the shared hostcall dispatcher (bd-1uy.1.1) so:
  - capability derivation + policy are consistent
  - timeouts/cancellation are enforced
  - error mapping uses taxonomy-only codes (`timeout|denied|io|invalid_request|internal`)

4) **Tests + E2E**
- Unit tests for each op (success + invalid_request + denied + timeout/cancel where applicable).
- Cross-mode parity tests (interactive vs RPC vs protocol adapter where applicable).
- E2E scenarios with JSONL logs + deterministic artifacts per bd-4u9.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Sample extensions that rely on session APIs run unmodified.
- Behavior is deterministic under harness control.
- Hostcall error codes are taxonomy-only.","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss
[ ] Unit tests cover core success/failure + edge cases for this bead
[ ] Integration/E2E script exercises the end-to-end path (or explicitly marks N/A with rationale in notes) and emits detailed JSONL logs + artifacts per bd-4u9
[ ] Logs include inputs, outputs, timing, and redaction summary; artifacts list is deterministic
[ ] Quality gates pass: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test
[ ] Docs/fixtures updated if behavior or UX changes","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T03:15:34.308214738Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T07:58:33.615194614Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["extensions","hostcall","interactive","rpc","session"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-321a","depends_on_id":"bd-1uy","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T03:15:34.315426860Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"bd-321a.1","title":"Spec: Extension session API contract (ops, params, outputs, determinism)","description":"# Goal
Define the **authoritative Extension Session connector contract** used by JS + WASM extensions.

This spec must be precise enough that we can implement it once and then:
- run conformance fixtures
- run cross-mode parity tests (interactive vs RPC)
- never re-open legacy code to resolve ambiguity

# Inputs (Where the truth lives)
- Legacy Pi (TypeScript): `legacy_pi_mono_code/` (search for `pi.session(`)
- Current Rust impl candidates:
  - `src/extensions.rs` (`dispatch_hostcall_session()`)
  - `src/interactive.rs` (`InteractiveExtensionSession`)
  - `src/session.rs` (`SessionHandle`)
  - `src/extension_dispatcher.rs` (table-driven tests + op coverage examples)

# Contract Surface
Pi extensions call `pi.session(op, args)`.

## Ops (must be specified)
Read-only:
- `get_state` (and accepted aliases)
- `get_messages`
- `get_entries`
- `get_branch`
- `get_model`
- `get_thinking_level`

Write:
- `set_name`
- `append_message`
- `append_entry` (custom entry)
- `set_model`
- `set_thinking_level`
- `set_label`

Convenience aliases (must be pinned):
- `get_file` (derived from state)
- `get_name` (derived from state)

## For each op, specify EXACTLY
- Allowed op spellings/aliases (case/underscore/camelCase tolerance)
- Params schema:
  - required keys
  - accepted alternate key spellings (e.g. `modelId` vs `model_id`)
  - defaults (if missing)
  - validation rules (empty string vs null vs missing)
- Output schema:
  - exact JSON shape (object keys + types)
  - nullability rules
  - ordering guarantees for lists
- Error mapping:
  - `invalid_request` for validation
  - `denied` when session handle is absent/unavailable
  - `io` for persistence failures
  - `internal` for unexpected failures
  - `timeout` only where the op is explicitly allowed to time out

# Canonical Output Shapes (must be stable)
## `get_state`
Pin the canonical state object.

Today interactive returns a rich object (model/thinking/streaming/session id/name/file/counts) while `SessionHandle` returns only `{sessionFile, sessionName}`.

This spec must choose the **canonical superset** and define default values for non-interactive/RPC mode so shape is identical across modes.

## Enumeration APIs
- Define whether `get_messages`/`get_entries` enumerate the **current branch/path** only (legacy-like) vs the entire session file.
- Define filtering rules for message types (which SessionMessage variants are included).

# Determinism Requirements
- For any list return value: define a stable ordering key (typically session order within the current path).
- Define whether IDs and timestamps are returned and if they need normalization in tests.

# Persistence Rules
Define when session mutations must be persisted:
- In interactive mode, persistence should occur immediately when `save_enabled`.
- In RPC mode, define whether persistence must be immediate or end-of-turn, and ensure it is identical to interactive in observable behavior.

# Hostcall ABI Integration Requirements
- Session ops must be expressible as canonical `HostCallPayload { capability: "session", method: "session", params: ... }`.
- The params shape must be compatible with `params_hash` parity across JS/WASM/protocol (bd-1uy.1.1.2).

# Deliverables
- A written spec section (likely in the bead description or referenced doc) listing:
  - op table with params/output/errors
  - canonical `get_state` schema
  - determinism + persistence rules
  - mapping into Hostcall ABI canonical params

## Acceptance Criteria
- Spec is detailed enough that two engineers can independently implement it and produce identical behavior.
- Spec explicitly calls out any deviations from legacy (ideally none).","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Op/params/output/error table complete for all session ops (including aliases)
[ ] Canonical `get_state` schema pinned with explicit defaults for non-interactive/RPC mode
[ ] Determinism rules explicit (ordering + filtering for messages/entries)
[ ] Persistence rules explicit (when and how session mutations are saved)
[ ] Canonical Hostcall ABI params shape pinned (enables `params_hash` parity)
[ ] Test plan enumerated: unit + parity + E2E (bd-1gl.1, bd-kh2.2)","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T08:00:31.904821722Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T08:05:50.956932462Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["determinism","extensions","hostcall","session","spec"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-321a.1","depends_on_id":"bd-321a","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T08:00:31.904821722Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

[ ] Test plan enumerated: unit + parity + E2E (bd-1gl.1, bd-kh2.2)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"RosePrairie","created_at":"2026-02-06T08:00:31.904821722Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T08:54:15.198473516Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T08:54:15.198444232Z","close_reason":"Spec complete (see comment)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["determinism","extensions","hostcall","session","spec"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-321a.1","depends_on_id":"bd-321a","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T08:00:31.904821722Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":529,"issue_id":"bd-321a.1","author":"RosePrairie","text":"# Extension Session API Contract (v1)

This is the **authoritative spec** for the extension session connector:

- Extension JS/WASM calls: `pi.session(op, args)`
- Hostcall ABI: `HostCallPayload { capability: "session", method: "session", params: { op, ... } }`
- Implementations MUST be **identical across modes** (interactive vs RPC/non-interactive), with explicit defaults where a mode lacks data.

Primary goals:
- Pin exact op names + aliases
- Pin params + output schemas (including ordering guarantees)
- Pin taxonomy-only error mapping
- Pin determinism + persistence rules
- Pin canonical ABI params shapes to enable `params_hash` parity

---

## 1) Canonical Hostcall Params Shape (Normative)

All session hostcalls use:

```json
{ "op": "get_state", "...": "..." }
```

Rules:
- `op` is required and MUST be a string.
- Host normalizes op by:
  - trimming
  - ASCII lowercasing
  - accepting both snake_case and camelCase spellings (see per-op alias lists).
- For `params_hash` parity, the **canonical** `op` value is the snake_case form listed below.
- Canonical argument keys are **camelCase** (e.g. `modelId`, `targetId`, `customType`).
  - Host MUST accept snake_case aliases.

---

## 2) Canonical `get_state` Schema (Normative)

`get_state` MUST return the legacy-compatible superset (matches pi-mono RPC `RpcSessionState`).

```jsonc
{
  // model may be omitted/null if not known
  "model": {
    "provider": "anthropic",
    "id": "claude-sonnet-4-20250514",
    "name": "Claude Sonnet 4",
    "api": "anthropic-messages",
    "baseUrl": "https://api.anthropic.com",
    "reasoning": true,
    "contextWindow": 200000,
    "maxTokens": 16384,
    "apiKeyPresent": true
  },

  "thinkingLevel": "off",          // string; default "off"
  "isStreaming": false,             // bool
  "isCompacting": false,            // bool
  "steeringMode": "one-at-a-time", // "all" | "one-at-a-time"
  "followUpMode": "one-at-a-time", // "all" | "one-at-a-time"

  "sessionFile": "/abs/path/session.jsonl", // string|null
  "sessionId": "<uuid>",                    // string (required)
  "sessionName": "my session",              // string|null

  "autoCompactionEnabled": false, // bool
  "messageCount": 0,              // int >= 0 (count on current branch)
  "pendingMessageCount": 0        // int >= 0
}
```

Defaults when the mode cannot supply a field:
- `model`: `null`
- `thinkingLevel`: `"off"`
- `isStreaming`: `false`
- `isCompacting`: `false`
- `steeringMode`: `"one-at-a-time"`
- `followUpMode`: `"one-at-a-time"`
- `sessionFile`: `null`
- `sessionId`: `""` only if session is unavailable (otherwise MUST be non-empty)
- `sessionName`: `null`
- `autoCompactionEnabled`: `false`
- `messageCount`: `0`
- `pendingMessageCount`: `0`

Notes:
- `messageCount` counts **SessionEntry::Message** entries on the **current branch**.
- `pendingMessageCount` is about queued steering/follow-up messages, not session file length.

---

## 3) Enumeration Semantics (Determinism)

### 3.1 `get_entries`
- Returns **all session entries** (entire session file), excluding the session header.
- Ordering: stable **append/file order** (oldest first).
- Includes all entry types: message, model_change, thinking_level_change, compaction, branch_summary, label, session_info, custom.

### 3.2 `get_branch`
- Returns the **current path** from root to current leaf (pi-mono `SessionManager.getBranch()` semantics).
- Ordering: stable path order (root → leaf).
- Includes all entry types in the path.

### 3.3 `get_messages`
- Returns message payloads for the **current branch only**.
- Include only `SessionEntry::Message` payloads whose `role` is one of:
  - `user`, `assistant`, `toolResult`, `custom`, `bashExecution`
- Exclude message roles `branchSummary` / `compactionSummary` (those are top-level entries).
- Ordering: branch order (root → leaf).

---

## 4) Op Table (Normative)

### Read ops

#### `get_state`
- Aliases accepted: `get_state`, `getState`, `getstate`
- Params: none
- Output: canonical `RpcSessionState` object (see §2)
- Errors:
  - `denied`: session handle unavailable

#### `get_entries`
- Aliases: `get_entries`, `getEntries`, `getentries`
- Output: `SessionEntry[]` (full file, append order)
- Errors:
  - `denied`: session handle unavailable

#### `get_branch`
- Aliases: `get_branch`, `getBranch`, `getbranch`
- Output: `SessionEntry[]` (current path)
- Errors:
  - `denied`: session handle unavailable

#### `get_messages`
- Aliases: `get_messages`, `getMessages`, `getmessages`
- Output: `SessionMessage[]` (current branch only; see §3.3)
- Errors:
  - `denied`: session handle unavailable

#### `get_model`
- Aliases: `get_model`, `getModel`, `getmodel`
- Output:
```json
{ "provider": "anthropic", "modelId": "claude-sonnet-4-20250514" }
```
- Nullability: either field may be `null`.
- Errors:
  - `denied`: session handle unavailable

#### `get_thinking_level`
- Aliases: `get_thinking_level`, `getThinkingLevel`, `getthinkinglevel`
- Output: string or `null`
- Errors:
  - `denied`: session handle unavailable

#### `get_file`
- Aliases: `get_file`, `getFile`, `getfile`
- Output: same as `get_state.sessionFile` (string|null)
- Errors:
  - `denied`: session handle unavailable

#### `get_name`
- Aliases: `get_name`, `getName`, `getname`
- Output: same as `get_state.sessionName` (string|null)
- Errors:
  - `denied`: session handle unavailable

---

### Write ops (append-only semantics)

Write ops MUST:
- append exactly one entry (except where explicitly defined otherwise)
- advance the session leaf pointer to the new entry
- persist immediately when persistence is enabled (see §5)
- return the new entry id as a string

#### `set_name`
- Aliases: `set_name`, `setName`, `setname`
- Canonical params:
```json
{ "name": "My Session" }
```
- Validation:
  - `name` required; if null/missing => `invalid_request`
  - empty/whitespace name => clear (store `null` name)
- Output: new entry id string (session_info entry)
- Errors:
  - `invalid_request`
  - `denied`
  - `io` (persistence failure)

#### `append_message`
- Aliases: `append_message`, `appendMessage`, `appendmessage`
- Canonical params:
```json
{ "message": { "role": "user", "content": "hi", "timestamp": 123 } }
```
- Compatibility:
  - If `message` field is missing, host MAY treat the entire params object as the message.
- Output: new entry id string (message entry)
- Errors:
  - `invalid_request` (message parse/validation)
  - `denied`
  - `io`

#### `append_entry`
- Aliases: `append_entry`, `appendEntry`, `appendentry`
- Canonical params:
```json
{ "customType": "my_ext.state", "data": {"k": "v"} }
```
- Validation:
  - `customType` required and non-empty
- Output: new entry id string (custom entry)
- Errors:
  - `invalid_request`
  - `denied`
  - `io`

#### `set_model`
- Aliases: `set_model`, `setModel`, `setmodel`
- Canonical params:
```json
{ "provider": "anthropic", "modelId": "claude-sonnet-4-20250514" }
```
- Validation: provider/modelId required non-empty
- Output: new entry id string (model_change entry)
- Side effects: update session header provider/modelId
- Errors: `invalid_request` | `denied` | `io`

#### `set_thinking_level`
- Aliases: `set_thinking_level`, `setThinkingLevel`, `setthinkinglevel`
- Canonical params:
```json
{ "thinkingLevel": "high" }
```
- Accepted aliases: `level`, `thinking_level`
- Validation: required non-empty
- Output: new entry id string (thinking_level_change entry)
- Side effects: update session header thinkingLevel
- Errors: `invalid_request` | `denied` | `io`

#### `set_label`
- Aliases: `set_label`, `setLabel`, `setlabel`
- Canonical params:
```json
{ "targetId": "abcd1234", "label": "review" }
```
- Accepted aliases:
  - `target_id`, `entryId`, `entry_id`
- Semantics:
  - `label` missing/null/empty => clear label for target
- Validation:
  - `targetId` required non-empty
  - targetId MUST exist in the session; otherwise `invalid_request`
- Output: new entry id string (label entry)
- Errors: `invalid_request` | `denied` | `io`

---

## 5) Persistence Rules (Normative)

- If the session is backed by a persistent store and saving is enabled:
  - every write op MUST call `save()` before returning success
  - failures surface as taxonomy `io`
- If saving is disabled (ephemeral sessions):
  - write ops mutate in-memory only and MUST NOT return `io` due to persistence

---

## 6) Error Taxonomy Mapping (Normative)

- `invalid_request`: bad `op`, missing/invalid params, parse failures, target missing
- `denied`: session handle not configured/unavailable
- `io`: persistence failure (save/index) or backing store I/O failures
- `internal`: invariant violations

Timeout:
- Session ops are expected to be fast; `timeout` should only arise from the outer hostcall deadline.

---

## 7) Known Rust Divergences to Fix (follow-up beads)

Current mismatches observed in Rust code (to be resolved in bd-321a.2/.3/.4):
- `SessionHandle.get_state()` currently returns only `{sessionFile, sessionName}`; must return the canonical superset (§2).
- `InteractiveExtensionSession.get_entries()` currently returns current path; must return full file entries (§3.1).
- `SessionHandle.get_messages()` currently iterates all entries; must be branch-only (§3.3).
- Write ops in `SessionHandle` currently do not persist immediately; must follow §5.
- `set_label` currently does not error when `targetId` does not exist; must be `invalid_request`.
- `append_entry` currently allows empty `customType`; must be `invalid_request`.

 {"id":"bd-321a.2","title":"Impl: Align SessionHandle (RPC/non-interactive) extension session semantics to spec","description":"# Goal
Make the non-interactive / RPC-mode session connector semantics match the session spec (bd-321a.1) exactly.

This work is primarily about the `SessionHandle` implementation in `src/session.rs`.

# Background (Observed)
`SessionHandle` currently diverges from interactive semantics:
- `get_state` returns only `{sessionFile, sessionName}`
- `get_messages` enumerates all `SessionEntry::Message` across the entire session (not branch-scoped)
- `get_entries` returns all entries (not branch-scoped)
- mutating ops do not persist (`save_enabled` not consulted)

# Scope
1) **Canonical state builder**
- Introduce a shared helper (module/function) that constructs the canonical `get_state` payload from:
  - Session header (provider/model/thinking)
  - Session entries for current path (messageCount)
  - Known runtime flags (streaming/compacting/pending message counts)
- The helper should be reusable by interactive and non-interactive implementations so drift becomes mechanically harder.

2) **Branch scoping + filtering**
- Update `get_messages` to follow the spec:
  - enumerate entries for current path
  - include exactly the allowed message variants
  - preserve stable order
- Update `get_entries` and `get_branch` semantics to match spec (likely both current-path scoped, unless spec chooses otherwise).

3) **Persistence semantics**
- Mutating ops must persist in the same cases as interactive.
- If the runtime has `save_enabled=false`, define the observable behavior and ensure it matches spec.

4) **Error mapping**
- Ensure any errors raised from session locking / save failures map onto the fixed hostcall taxonomy (no ad-hoc codes).

# Tests
- Unit tests for `SessionHandle` op semantics:
  - `get_state` schema keys + defaults
  - `get_messages` filtering and ordering
  - `get_entries`/`get_branch` scoping
  - persistence: verify session file changes when `save_enabled=true`

## Acceptance Criteria
- Running the same extension in interactive and RPC mode yields identical session API observable outputs (modulo explicitly-specified fields).
- No new behavior is introduced outside the spec.","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss
[ ] `SessionHandle` behavior matches the spec (bd-321a.1) for all implemented ops
[ ] Unit tests cover success/failure + edge cases for all touched ops
[ ] Integration/E2E coverage added or updated (bd-1gl.1 and/or bd-kh2.2) with JSONL logs + artifacts per bd-4u9
[ ] Logs include inputs, outputs, timing, and redaction summary; artifact index deterministic
[ ] Quality gates pass: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T08:00:57.344850651Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T08:06:02.426091909Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["extensions","implementation","persistence","rpc","session"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-321a.2","depends_on_id":"bd-321a","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T08:00:57.344850651Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-321a.3","title":"Impl: Align InteractiveExtensionSession extension session semantics to spec","description":"# Goal
Audit and align `InteractiveExtensionSession` (`src/interactive.rs`) with the session spec (bd-321a.1), and eliminate any remaining drift vs RPC/non-interactive.

# Background (Observed)
Interactive already has the richer and likely-legacy-correct behavior, but it still needs to be pinned to the spec so it cannot accidentally diverge.

Key risk areas:
- Canonical `get_state` schema: fields, defaults, and naming
- Enumeration semantics: which entries/messages are included, and branch/path scoping
- Persistence + error mapping behavior

# Scope
1) **Adopt shared canonical state builder**
- Switch interactive `get_state` construction to use the shared helper introduced in bd-321a.2 (or equivalent).
- Any interactive-only fields must be explicitly justified in the spec; otherwise they must be provided in RPC mode too.

2) **Enumeration semantics**
- Ensure `get_messages`/`get_entries`/`get_branch` match spec exactly.
- Confirm deterministic ordering and stable filtering across runs.

3) **Persistence**
- Verify all mutating ops persist when `save_enabled=true`.

4) **Error taxonomy**
- Ensure failures map to taxonomy-only codes via the shared hostcall dispatcher once session is routed through it (bd-321a.4).

# Tests
- Unit tests focused on interactive semantics:
  - `get_state` schema stability
  - enumeration output stability
  - persistence and save behavior

## Acceptance Criteria
- Interactive mode matches the spec and matches RPC mode for all session connector ops.","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss
[ ] `InteractiveExtensionSession` behavior matches the spec (bd-321a.1) for all implemented ops
[ ] Cross-mode parity verified vs `SessionHandle` (bd-321a.2)
[ ] Unit tests cover success/failure + edge cases
[ ] E2E coverage added or updated (bd-1gl.1 and/or bd-kh2.2) with JSONL logs + artifacts per bd-4u9
[ ] Quality gates pass: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T08:01:17.770046994Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T08:06:13.631770411Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["extensions","implementation","interactive","session"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-321a.3","depends_on_id":"bd-321a","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T08:01:17.770046994Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-321a.4","title":"Hostcall ABI: Session method routing uses shared dispatcher + taxonomy","description":"# Goal
Route all extension session operations through the **shared hostcall dispatcher** (bd-1uy.1.1) so behavior is:
- consistent across JS / WASM / protocol adapter
- capability-enforced in one place
- taxonomy-correct (`timeout|denied|io|invalid_request|internal` only)
- conformance-testable with stable `params_hash` parity

# Background (Observed)
Session ops currently have logic in `dispatch_hostcall_session()` (`src/extensions.rs`) and differ between interactive and non-interactive `ExtensionSession` implementations.

The Hostcall ABI workstream requires:
- canonical params shape
- strict error taxonomy
- shared enforcement point

# Scope
1) **Canonical params shape**
- Implement the spec-chosen canonical `HostCallPayload` params for `method="session"` (bd-321a.1).
- Ensure JS/WASM/protocol compute identical `params_hash` for equivalent calls.

2) **Dispatcher wiring**
- Ensure the runtime path that handles session hostcalls calls `dispatch_host_call_shared()` (bd-1uy.1.1).
- Any existing direct dispatch must be removed or made a thin adapter.

3) **Taxonomy-only errors**
- Eliminate any ad-hoc error codes observable at the ABI boundary.
- Ensure "no session configured" is `denied` (not `invalid_request`).

4) **Parity tests integration**
- Extend (or satisfy) bd-1uy.1.4 canonical cases for session:
  - `get_state` success
  - `set_model` invalid_request (missing provider/modelId)
  - `set_label` invalid_request (missing targetId)
  - denied when session handle missing

# Tests
- Table-driven unit tests for the dispatcher session route.
- Parity test coverage via bd-1uy.1.4.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Session hostcalls behave identically across JS/WASM/protocol adapter and emit taxonomy-only errors.","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Session hostcalls route through the shared dispatcher (bd-1uy.1.1)
[ ] Canonical params shape implemented and `params_hash` parity holds across runtimes
[ ] Only taxonomy error codes are observable at the ABI boundary
[ ] Parity test suite (bd-1uy.1.4) includes session cases and passes
[ ] Unit tests cover success + error paths for session routing
[ ] Quality gates pass: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T08:01:37.757846678Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T08:06:25.691069920Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["extensions","hostcall","session","taxonomy","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-321a.4","depends_on_id":"bd-1uy.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T08:01:37.757846678Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-321a.4","depends_on_id":"bd-321a","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T08:01:37.757846678Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-321a.4","depends_on_id":"bd-321a.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T08:01:37.757846678Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
